stem: Hexadecimal is a base-16 number system used to write binary values more concisely.
model_answer: Give a valid 2-digit hex example (e.g. 2A). Either: multiply the left digit by 16 and add the value of the right digit (2*16 + 10 = 42). Or: convert each digit to its 4-bit binary (2 = 0010, A = 1010), combine to make an 8-bit binary number (00101010), then convert that to denary (42).
review_notes: 2024 J277/01 Q1(d). No marks for denary->hex. Method marks still available if the worked example is numerically wrong.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe how to convert a 2-digit hexadecimal number into denary. Use an example in your answer.

mark_points.text: gives a valid 2-digit hexadecimal example and correctly converts it to denary
multiply left/first digit by 16; or convert each hex digit to 4-bit binary
add the value of the right/second digit; or combine to 8-bit binary and convert to denary

misconceptions.label: Converts the wrong way
misconceptions.description: Some pupils describe denary-to-hex instead of hex-to-denary; read the command word carefully.
